Article.ArticleTitle: The Thin Line Between Waking and Sleeping in Athletes: A Call for Yoga Nidra in the Sporting Context.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Department of Medicine and Aging Sciences, Behavioral Imaging and Neural Dynamics Center, University "G. d'Annunzio" of Chieti-Pescara, Chieti, Italy.
Department of Medicine and Aging Sciences, Behavioral Imaging and Neural Dynamics Center, University "G. d'Annunzio" of Chieti-Pescara, Chieti, Italy.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: The authors declare that the research was conducted in the absence of any commercial or financial relationships that could be construed as a potential conflict of interest.

Reference.Citation: Balk Y. A., Englert C. (2020). Recovery self-regulation in sport: theory, research, and practice. Int. J. Sports Sci. Coach. 15, 273–281. 10.1177/1747954119897528
Cebolla A. M., Cheron G. (2019). Understanding neural oscillations in the human brain: from movement to consciousness and vice versa. Front. Psychol. 10:1930. 10.3389/fpsyg.2019.01930

Cramer H., Lauche R., Langhorst J., Dobos G. (2016). Is one yoga style better than another? A systematic review of associations of yoga style and conclusions in randomized yoga trials. Compl. Ther. Med. 25, 178–187. 10.1016/j.ctim.2016.02.015
Datta K., Kumar A., Sekar C. (2020). Enhancement of performance in an elite archer after non-pharmacological intervention to improve sleep. Med. J. Arm. Forc. India 76, 338–341.
Datta K., Tripathi M., Mallick H. N. (2017). Yoga Nidra: an innovative approach for management of chronic insomnia-A case report. Sleep Sci. Pract. 1, 1–11. 10.1186/s41606-017-0009-4

di Fronso S., Werthner P., Christie S., Bertollo M. (2020). “Using technology for self-regulation in sport,” in Feelings in Sport: Theory, Research, and Practical Implications for Performance and Well-being, eds Ruiz M. C., Robazza C. (New York, NY: Taylor & Francis; ), 178–186. 10.4324/9781003052012-19
Diener E., Suh E. M., Lucas R. E., Smith H. L. (1999). Subjective well-being: three decades of progress. Psychol. Bull. 125, 276–302. 10.1037/0033-2909.125.2.276
Dol K. S. (2019). Effects of a yoga nidra on the life stress and self-esteem in university students. Complem. Ther. Clin. Prac. 35, 232–236. 10.1016/j.ctcp.2019.03.004

Goodman F. R., Kashdan T. B., Mallard T. T., Schumann M. (2014). A brief mindfulness and yoga intervention with an entire NCAA Division I athletic team: an initial investigation. Psychol. Consc. Theory Res. Pract. 1, 339–356. 10.1037/cns0000022
Gupta L., Morgan K., North C., Gilchrist S. (2020). Napping in high-performance athletes: sleepiness or sleepability? Eur. J. Sport Sci. 1–10. 10.1080/17461391.2020.1743765

Gutmann B., Zimmer P., Hülsdünker T., Lefebvre J., Binnebößel S., Oberste M., et al. (2018). The effects of exercise intensity and post-exercise recovery time on cortical activation as revealed by EEG alpha peak frequency. Neurosci. Lett. 668, 159–163. 10.1016/j.neulet.2018.01.007

Kellmann M., Bertollo M., Bosquet L., Brink M., Coutts A. J., Duffield R., et al. (2018). Recovery and performance in sport: consensus statement. Int. J. Sports Physiol. Perform. 13, 240–245. 10.1123/ijspp.2017-0759

Kumar K., Joshi B. (2009). Study on the effect of Pranakarshan pranayama and yoga nidra on alpha EEG & GSR. Indian J. Trad. Knowl. 8, 453–454. Available online at: http://nopr.niscair.res.in/handle/123456789/5093

Loch F., Ferrauti A., Meyer T., Pfeiffer M., Kellmann M. (2019). Resting the mind–a novel topic with scarce insights. Considering potential mental recovery strategies for short rest periods in sports. Perf. Enhance. Health 6, 148–155. 10.1016/j.peh.2019.04.002

Markil N., Whitehurst M., Jacobs P. L., Zoeller R. F. (2012). Yoga nidra relaxation increases heart rate variability and is unaffected by a prior bout of Hatha yoga. J. Altern. Compl. Med. 18, 953–958. 10.1089/acm.2011.0331

Moszeik E. N., von Oertzen T., Renner K. H. (2020). Effectiveness of a short yoga nidra meditation on stress, sleep, and well-being in a large and diverse sample. Curr. Psychol. 1–15. 10.1007/s12144-020-01042-2
Park C. L., Finkelstein-Fox L., Sacco S. J., Braun T. D., Lazar S. (2020). How does yoga reduce stress? A clinical trial testing psychological mechanism. Stress Health 36, 330–337. 10.1002/smi.2977

Parker S., Bharati S. V., Fernandez M. (2013). Defining yoga-nidra: traditional accounts, physiological research, and future directions. Int. J. Yoga Ther. 23, 11–16. 10.17761/ijyt.23.1.t636651v22018148

Pelka M., Heidari J., Ferrauti A., Meyer T., Pfeiffer M., Kellmann M. (2016). Relaxation techniques in sports: a systematic review on acute effects on performance. Perf. Enhance. Health 5, 47–59. 10.1016/j.peh.2016.05.003
Saraswati S. S. (2009). Yoga Nidr, 4th Edn. Munger: Yoga Publication Trust

Stankovic L. (2011). Transforming trauma: a qualitative feasibility study of integrative restoration (iRest) yoga nidra on combat related post-traumatic stress disorder. Int. J. Yoga Ther. 21, 23–37. 10.17761/ijyt.21.1.v823454h5v57n160
